title: Running the Night in ShiftBuilder
eyebrow: Gun Lake Operations · Grave Shift · Deployment Board
footer_note: ShiftBuilder Guide · Gun Lake Casino Resort · Operations. The on-screen board is the live Zone Deployment Sheet; for the reading standard, see IM-SOP-600, Reading the Grave Zone Deployment Sheet.

blocks.text: ShiftBuilder is where you read and run the nightly Grave Zone Deployment Sheet. The board on your screen is the live sheet: what you change is the real assignment the moment you make it. This guide covers signing in, finding the night, reading the board, covering gaps, the Overlaps page, and printing.

blocks.label: The One Rule
blocks.text: The board is live. What you change is the real assignment the instant you make it: there is no separate save step.
Signing In

items: Open ShiftBuilder. The lock screen reads ShiftBuilder Access.
Enter your 6-digit ops PIN. It submits on the sixth digit; Clear starts over.
On SIGNED IN, the board opens on tonight's Grave date. The first time you sign in, the Grave Cover Guide walks you through the board once; reopen it any time from the (⋯) menu.

blocks.text: Finding the Night
You see a night once it has been published. Use the week strip or the calendar to land on the night you need, or tap Today to jump to tonight.

blocks.html: 1JULY 2026 ▾2◎ Today3F S ●25 M T W T  ‹ ›4☰ Deployment | Overlap5● PUBLISHED6BK   ⋯

items: Month and date. Click the month (for example, JULY 2026) to open the calendar and jump to any date.
Today. Snaps you to tonight's Grave date.
The week strip. Seven day pills for the current Grave week; the arrows step to the previous or next week. The highlighted pill is the night you are viewing.
Roster and view. Show or hide the Team roster, and use the layers icon to flip between the Deployment board and the Overlap sheet.
The night. A published night shows as posted and live. That is the night you work.
Your menu. Your initials open your account (Sign out). The (⋯) menu holds Refresh Day, the Grave Cover Guide, and Print.

blocks.text: Reading the Board

The board is the Zone Deployment Sheet, live. It reads top to bottom, the same as the printed book: ZONES, then RESTROOMS, then AUXILIARY, each with its fill count (for example, ZONES 5 / 10 FILLED). For the full reading standard, use IM-SOP-600.

blocks.side_html:
● ZONE 7
2
Marcus
Zone 7 Smoking Room
Zone 7 Self Serve Station
+ ZONE 2
items: Accent bar and label. The color and zone symbol are the area's fixed identity, the same as on the printed sheet.
Corner mark. The badge in the top-right is the break group (1, 2, 3, or OL).
The name. Who owns the area tonight, or “Unfilled” when the slot is open.
Location lines. The area's posts and task notes, in muted gray.
Coverage band. A colored band names an area this person also carries (see Section 05).

blocks.text: Working a Card

A card has two live regions. Double-click the name band at the top of a card to open its Placement Pad. Double-click the task area, the lines below the name, to open the Tasks Pad (Section 07). The Placement Pad is the per-person control panel; top to bottom it holds:

items: Header. The Team Member’s name and the area chip in the area’s own identity color; an open slot shows Unassigned.
Insight line. When available, a one-sentence rotation verdict for tonight, built from the same facts as the matrix: spread, gaps, and this week’s pattern.
Tasks and sweeper. A quick add-task row and the Assign Sweeper button (Section 07).
The Placement Matrix and LAST 5. The placement memory (Section 06).
Action bar. Lock, Clear, Coverage, Swap along the bottom.

blocks.text: The three actions you run the night with:

rows: Put a different Team Member in the slot, or fill an open slot.
Remove the Team Member; the slot goes open.
Have this person also carry another area. See Section 07.

blocks.label: Locked cards
blocks.text: A card marked Locked is pinned for the night: Swap and Clear will not change it, and the board will refuse the edit. Leave the Lock button alone; it is not part of running the night. If the whole night refuses changes, the night itself is locked: read and print only.
Filling a Slot

Tap Swap on an assigned card, or open an Unfilled card, and the Team Member picker opens. It lists only people who can hold that area tonight: scheduled, the right section, and, for restroom sides, the matching gender. Whoever you tap takes the slot immediately: the board is live.
Every name in the picker carries a fit dot: a one-glance verdict on how tonight’s placement sits with that person’s rotation.

blocks.html: BestOkayCheckCriticalInvalid  tap any dot for the verdict and the facts behind it

items: Best (green). Rotation supports it: little or no recent exposure, no same-week repeat.
Okay (cyan). Workable. Some recent exposure, nothing alarming.
Check (orange). Look before you tap: repeat exposure building up or a same-week repeat on this slot.
Critical (dark red). A heavy repeat: this placement stacks straight onto recent nights in the same area.
Invalid (red). The placement does not work as the board stands; pick someone else or fix the conflict first.

blocks.text: Tap the dot itself for the verdict popover: the label, a one-line summary, and the fact line behind it. The dot is advice built from placement history; you place the person.
The Placement Matrix

Below the pad’s actions sits the Placement Matrix: the Team Member’s placement memory. Open it the same way, double-click the name band of their card. Check it before you Swap someone in, approve a swap, or add coverage: it tells you where they have been, how often, and how recently.

blocks.html:

Marcus’s matrix, read in five seconds: Zone 7 three or more times in his last 30 nights worked (red), Zone 3 and Restroom 7 Men’s twice (orange), healthy green and gray everywhere else, and 12 days since Zone 9. Tonight is not a Zone 7 night for Marcus.

blocks.text: The spread window. The matrix counts the Team Member’s last 30 grave nights actually worked, before the night you are building. Nights off do not shrink the window; it is their nights, not the calendar. The counts row shows how many restroom sides and zones this person can hold (restroom sides match gender), plus days since their last Zone 9 and Zone 9 Smoking Room placement: 12d means twelve days ago, and a dash means never on record.
The grid shows one pill per area, colored by how many of those 30 nights the person spent there:

blocks.html:
PillWhat it meansHow to act
GrayZero nights in that area within their last 30 nights worked. Not in the spread at all.An open lane for rotation, but gray is not a qualification: confirm they can hold the area before you treat it as an option.
Green · 1×Worked that area exactly once in the window.Healthy rotation. Greens are your first-choice lanes when moving people.
Orange · 2×Worked it twice in the window.Caution. Fine when the night demands it, but look for a green or gray first.
Red · 3×+Three or more nights there in the window. Heavy recent exposure.Do not stack another night on it without a reason. Reds are where rotation complaints and skill fade come from.

blocks.text: LAST 5 uses a different color language on purpose. Its pills are the five most recent placements, newest first, repeats included, and each pill wears its area’s identity color, the same color that area’s card wears on the board. So the grid answers how often, in frequency colors; LAST 5 answers where, lately, in area colors. Three blue Z7 pills in a row is a rotation problem you can see from across the room.

One caution: the matrix is history, not eligibility. A gray pill means no recent exposure; it does not mean the person is trained for that area, and the picker’s eligibility rules still apply either way.
Working the Tasks
Task lines are the duties printed on a card under the name. Double-click the task area of a card to open the Tasks Pad and edit them:

items: Add a task. Type the line and add it. It prints on the card exactly as written, so write it the way the floor should read it (for example, “Detail slot bases, rows E and F”).
Use the recent chips. The pad shows recently used task lines as one-tap chips: tap one to add it without retyping.
Remove a task. Tap the ✕ on the task line.
Assign the sweeper. Tap Assign Sweeper and pick Sweep 5/8/HL or Sweep 9/10/SR. The button grays out once that card carries a sweep.

blocks.label: Before you print
blocks.text: Both sweeps, Sweep 5/8/HL and Sweep 9/10/SR, must be owned by someone every night. If neither appears on any card, assign them before the book prints.
Covering a Gap
When an area has no one, you do not edit the empty area. You assign the coverage from the card of the person who will carry it.

items: Double-click the card of the person who will cover the gap.
In the placement pad, tap Coverage.
Under Add coverage, pick the area they will carry from Zones, Restrooms, or Aux / Support. For anything else, type it in the Custom text field (for example, “High Limit Slots”) and tap Add.
A coverage band appears at the bottom of that person's card, naming the area in its color (for example, + ZONE 2). To undo it, tap the ✕ on the band.

blocks.label: Where the Band Lives
blocks.text: The coverage band always sits on the coverer's card, never on the covered area's card. That is how the board shows one person carrying two areas at once.
The Overlaps Page

Tap the layers icon in the navigation to open the Overlap sheet. It lays the night out by break wave (groups 1, 2, 3, and OL) and shows the PM and AM overlap crews. It works exactly like the deployment board: double-click a card to Swap, Clear, or add Coverage. Tap the layers icon again to return to the board.
Printing

Open the (⋯) menu and choose Print. ShiftBuilder lays out the Zone Deployment Sheet and the Overlap sheet. Set the options as needed: Official prints the full sheet, Planning leaves slots blank; you can set page order, margins, and a cover page. View Print Preview shows the print layout on screen first.
The Quick Routine

items: Sign in with your PIN.
Open tonight's published night.
Read the board top to bottom: ZONES, RESTROOMS, AUXILIARY.
Swap in replacements and cover any gaps.
Add or adjust task lines and make sure both sweeps are owned.
Check the Overlap sheet.
Print the book for the floor.
